general_standards.listening: Can identify sounds of familiar words and basic phrases spoken slowly and clearly.
general_standards.reading: Can recognize familiar words in simple texts.
general_standards.speaking: Can use basic language and formulaic expressions to describe things and answer questions.
general_standards.writing: Can write about oneself using simple words.
general_standards.mediation: Can use mediation strategies such as gestures and body language to express basic ideas.

scenarios.category: Expressing Ourselves and Connecting with Others

listening.general_standard: Can identify sounds of familiar words and basic phrases spoken slowly and clearly.
specific_standards: Receptive: Recognize familiar words and basic phrases.
Interactive: Respond to simple conversations on familiar topics by using gestures or by pointing.
learning_outcomes: Can recognize familiar words related to days of the week spoken slowly (e.g., "Monday." "Today is Monday." "It's Monday.").
Can respond to the basic days of the week and weekend by pointing at the word or position on a basic calendar.
Can recognize and respond to questions about the days of the week using basic phrases (e.g., "Is today Tuesday?" "No, it is Saturday." "It is the weekend.").

reading.general_standard: Can recognize familiar words in simple texts.
specific_standards: Reading: Find familiar words in a text.
Reading: Identify one-step written directions with visual support.
Phonemic Awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.
learning_outcomes: Can find and point to familiar words in simple texts (e.g., "Monday," "Tuesday").

Can identify and follow one-step written directions with visual support (e.g., following the instruction, "Point to the word 'Monday'" in the sentence "Today is Monday," supported by a calendar picture).

Can identify the sounds of the letters of the alphabet in familiar vocabulary related to days of the week and daily routines when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound (e.g., identifying the sound /w/ in "Wednesday" supported with a picture).

speaking.general_standard: Can use basic language and formulaic expressions to describe things and answer questions.
specific_standards: Productive: Use basic words or phrases to identify time words.
Interactive: Exchange simple questions and answers about the current day.
learning_outcomes: Can identify specific time-related words, such as days of the week and the weekend, in simple sentences (e.g., "Saturday and Sunday are the weekends." "Today is Tuesday.").
Can exchange simple questions and answers about the current day, using basic time-related words (e.g., "What day is it today?" "Today is Monday.").
Can respond to questions about time using basic words and phrases to indicate days (e.g., "What is tomorrow?" "Tomorrow is Wednesday." or "It's the weekend.").

writing.general_standard: Can write about oneself using simple words.
specific_standards: Productive: Write their own name and other very familiar words without assistance.
Interactive: Draw pictures related to a simple sentence they have written.
learning_outcomes: Can write their name and days of the week without assistance (e.g., "Yamileth" and "Tuesday").
Can write simple greetings on cards (e.g., "Happy Monday!").
Can draw pictures for sentences they have written (e.g., a greeting card for the sentence "Happy Monday!").

mediation.general_standard: Can use mediation strategies such as gestures and body language to express basic ideas.
specific_standards: Text: Use pictures or objects to express ideas.
Concept: Use facial expressions and simple gestures to help explain feelings or needs.
learning_outcomes: Can use simple drawings or pictures to communicate days of the week to peers (e.g., drawing a picture of a calendar showing the days of the week).
Can use gestures and facial expressions to express basic ideas about days of the week to others (e.g., pointing to a picture representing a specific day).
Can clarify misunderstandings about days of the week using body language (e.g., pointing to the correct day on a calendar).

pronunciation_and_phonemic_awareness.phonemic_awareness: Recognizing sounds of letters of the alphabet in familiar words related to days of the week and routines

pragmatic: Exchanging questions and answers about the current day using simple phrases like "Today is Monday."
Using time-related vocabulary: Identifying words for days and weekends by using or pointing to them
sociolinguistic: Classroom schedules: Using polite expressions when talking about the days of the week and routines.

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Days of the Week Calendar
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students create a visual calendar to reinforce their understanding of the days of the week. They mark and label each day, including weekdays and weekends, and illustrate common daily activities.

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Day of the Week Song
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students create and sing a song about the days of the week using simple sentences like "Today is Monday."

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Daily Schedule Chart
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students create a chart outlining their activities each day of the week, labeling each activity with simple sentences like "On Monday, I play."

skills: Time Awareness: Recognizing days of the week.
Communication: Using days of the week to communicate calendar time.
technology: Use an online calendar tool to fill in days and activities.

listening.general_standard: Can identify sounds of familiar words and basic phrases spoken slowly and clearly.
specific_standards: Receptive: Follow very simple directions and instructions.
Interactive: Respond to simple conversations on familiar topics by using gestures or by pointing.
learning_outcomes: Can follow basic instructions related to weather (e.g., "Look outside, it's sunny.").
Can recognize weather-related words like "sunny" or "raining" in conversations.
Can respond to questions about the weather (e.g., "What is the weather today?").

reading.general_standard: Can recognize familiar words in simple texts.
specific_standards: Reading: Identify one-step written directions with visual support.
Reading: Match simple words to corresponding pictures.
Phonemic Awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.
learning_outcomes: Can identify weather-related words in simple text instructions (e.g., matching "sun" to a picture of the sun).
Can match simple weather-related words to corresponding pictures (e.g., matching the word "sunny" to a picture of a sunny day).

Can identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound in familiar words related to weather (e.g., identifying the sound of the letter /r/ in the word "rainy" when shown a picture of raindrops).

speaking.general_standard: Can use basic language and formulaic expressions to describe things and answer questions.
specific_standards: Productive: Use basic words or phrases to identify time words.
Interactive: Exchange simple questions and answers about the current day.
learning_outcomes: Can count weather features up to 20, with help (e.g., "Let's count the clouds! One, two, three.").
Can answer simple questions about the weather, using one- or two-word responses (e.g., responding "cold" when asked, "What is the weather today?").
Can express simple feelings or reactions to weather using words and gestures (e.g., saying "I'm hot." while fanning themselves or "I'm cold." with a shivering gesture).

writing.general_standard: Can write about oneself using simple words.
specific_standards: Productive: Fill in a form with their name and age with adult help.
Interactive: Write simple phrases with errors on a drawing or postcard.
learning_outcomes: Can write their name and simple phrases on a weather-related drawing (e.g., labeling a personal drawing of a sunny day with their name and a simple phrase describing the drawing).
Can write simple phrases related to the weather (e.g., "It is sunny.") with support.
Can label pictures of different weather conditions (e.g., drawing a cloud and writing "cloudy").

mediation.general_standard: Can use mediation strategies such as gestures and body language to express basic ideas.
specific_standards: Text: Draw a single picture that represents a simple story or event.
Communication: Use gestures to show how to play a very simple game.
learning_outcomes: Can clarify or confirm understanding of weather by pointing to pictures or using simple words.
Can draw simple pictures representing the weather and label them with help.
Can use gestures or body language to describe weather (e.g., pretending to shiver when it's cold).

pronunciation_and_phonemic_awareness.phonemic_awareness: Initial sound of letter /w/ in the word "weather" and identification of sounds of letters of the alphabet in familiar vocabulary (e.g., /r/ in "rainy")

pragmatic: Talking about weather: Describe simple weather conditions (e.g., "It is sunny.") and describe simple reactions to weather (e.g., "I'm cold." or "I'm hot.")
Asking questions about weather: Ask basic questions, such as "What is the weather today?" to learn about daily conditions
sociolinguistic: Weather conversations: Engaging in simple weather-related conversations.

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Weather Wheel
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Children create a "weather wheel" with pictures of sunny, rainy, and windy weather and use it to describe the daily weather in class.

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Weather Journal
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students maintain a weather journal where they draw the day's weather and write sentences like "It is sunny.".

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Weather Report Video
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students act as weather reporters and record short videos describing the weather using sentences like "It is raining today.".

skills: Critical Thinking: Observing and describing weather.
technology: Use an interactive weather app to learn about different types of weather.

listening.general_standard: Can identify sounds of familiar words and basic phrases spoken slowly and clearly.
specific_standards: Receptive: Identify the topic of a conversation that involves familiar subjects.
Interactive: Respond to simple conversations on familiar topics by using gestures or by pointing.
learning_outcomes: Can show comprehension of age-appropriate numbers when paired with familiar objects (e.g., identifying "three blue flowers" by pointing to the correct group).
Can identify colors and objects when spoken about (e.g., "blue bird" or "red flower").
Can follow simple instructions related to colors and familiar objects by pointing or gesturing (e.g., "Find the blue bird.").

reading.general_standard: Can recognize familiar words in simple texts.
specific_standards: Reading: Identify pictures that show what happened in a simple story heard.
Reading: Identify simple words for very common symbols.
Phonemic Awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.
learning_outcomes: Can listen to a simple story and identify corresponding pictures that illustrate key events (e.g., recognizing the picture of a blue bird mentioned in the story).
Can identify simple words for very common symbols, matching them to images (e.g., linking "flower" with its picture).
Can identify the initial sounds of letters of the alphabet in common objects and things (e.g., /f/ in "fish," /b/ in "bird," and /k/ in "cat").

speaking.general_standard: Can use basic language and formulaic expressions to describe things and answer questions.
specific_standards: Productive: Name primary and secondary colors.
Interactive: Express likes or dislikes using simple words and pointing.
learning_outcomes: Can describe objects using colors and simple sentences (e.g., "That's a blue bird.").
Can answer questions about colors and objects, using one or two-word responses (e.g., "What color is it?" "Blue.").
Can express likes and dislikes, using simple words and gestures (e.g., "I like red.").

writing.general_standard: Can write about oneself using simple words.
specific_standards: Productive: Write numbers up to 10 with guidance.
Interactive: Write one-word answers to simple questions.
learning_outcomes: Can write numbers up to ten with teacher guidance to represent objects in drawings (e.g., drawing four flowers and writing "4").
Can respond to simple questions about objects or colors by writing one-word answers with teacher support (e.g., "What color is this?" Write "blue.").
Can use simple drawings to communicate about objects and add one-word descriptions (e.g., drawing a green leaf and writing "leaf").

mediation.general_standard: Can use mediation strategies such as gestures and body language to express basic ideas.
specific_standards: Text: Identify a few familiar words from a story by connecting them to prior knowledge.
Concept: Use a word and a gesture or drawing to describe a familiar object.
learning_outcomes: Can use simple drawings to communicate about objects (e.g., drawing a blue bird).
Can use gestures or point to objects to clarify their responses when describing colors.
Can describe objects and concepts from a story by connecting prior knowledge with supportive strategies, such as using words and gestures (e.g., saying "bird" while flapping arms).

grammatical_features: Present simple (e.g., "It is an orange cat.")

pronunciation_and_phonemic_awareness.phonemic_awareness: Identifying initial sounds of letters of the alphabet in common objects and things (e.g., /f/ in "fish," /b/ in "bird," /k/ in "cat")

pragmatic: Recognizing colors and objects: Identify familiar objects by color (e.g., "blue bird" or "red flower.")
Describing with colors: Use simple color phrases in sentences (e.g., "That is a red bird.")
Counting and identifying colors of objects: Identify and count familiar objects while specifying their color (e.g., "three blue birds" or "two red flowers")
sociolinguistic: Observing surroundings: Describing objects using color and shape while considering their cultural or personal significance.

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Color Nature Walk
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students go on a "color nature walk" around the school, observing and collecting colorful objects (e.g., blue flowers, green leaves), then create a color chart.

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Color Sorting Game
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students play a color sorting game matching colored objects (e.g., blue birds, red cars) to their corresponding colors on a chart.

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Color Collage
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students create a collage using colored paper or magazine cutouts, labeling objects with sentences like "This is a blue bird."

technology: Use a tablet to take photos of objects and create a digital color chart with the teacher's help.

listening.general_standard: Can identify sounds of familiar words and basic phrases spoken slowly and clearly.
specific_standards: Receptive: Show comprehension of basic expressions for greeting and leave-taking if supported by gestures.
Interactive: Answer simple questions about oneself, using gestures or formulaic expressions.
learning_outcomes: Can listen for and understand words related to greetings, leave-takings, and family members (e.g., "hello," "goodbye," "mom," "dad").
Can follow instructions about family-related activities (e.g., "Find a picture of your family.").
Can respond to questions about family members, using gestures or single words (e.g., "Who is he?" "Dad").

reading.general_standard: Can recognize familiar words in simple texts.
specific_standards: Reading: Recognize when written, without visual support, not only their own name but also the names of classmates or family members.
Reading: Point to simple words in the text that identify objects or characters in a picture book.
Phonemic Awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.
learning_outcomes: Can recognize family-related words, including own name, in written texts (e.g., "mom," "dad").
Can match names of family members to pictures in books (e.g., matching the word "father" to a picture of a father in a family picture book).

Can identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound (e.g., identifying the sound of the letter "S" in the word "sister" when shown a picture of a sister).

speaking.general_standard: Can use basic language and formulaic expressions to describe things and answer questions.
specific_standards: Productive: Locate objects and people using words.
Interactive: Respond nonverbally to simple yes/no questions by nodding or shaking head.
learning_outcomes: Can identify family members, using simple sentences (e.g., "She is my mom.").
Can answer basic yes/no questions about family members, using nods, gestures, or simple words (e.g., "Is she your mom?").
Can participate in conversations about their family, using one-word responses (e.g., "Who is she?" "Mom.") or two-word responses (e.g., "Who is she?" "My sister.").

writing.general_standard: Can write about oneself using simple words.
specific_standards: Productive: Write their own name and other very familiar words without assistance.
Interactive: Draw pictures related to a simple sentence they have written.
learning_outcomes: Can write other very familiar family-related words without assistance when labeling a family drawing (e.g., "mom," "dad").
Can draw pictures related to simple sentences they have written about family members (e.g., drawing a picture of "my mom" to accompany the sentence, "This is my mom.").
Can write familiar family words on cards, without assistance.

mediation.general_standard: Can use mediation strategies such as gestures and body language to express basic ideas.
specific_standards: Text: Use pictures or objects to express ideas.
Communication: Use gestures and demonstrations to help classmates perform a simple task.
learning_outcomes: Can use pictures and gestures to describe their family to classmates (e.g., miming or displaying a drawing or picture of a family member).
Can clarify responses by pointing to family pictures or objects (e.g., pointing to a picture of their mother).
Can use simple gestures or facial expressions to show who they are talking about in their family (e.g., pointing to a family member's picture).

pronunciation_and_phonemic_awareness.phonemic_awareness: Matching letter sounds to words, such as /b/ in "brother" and /s/ in "sister"

pragmatic: Introducing family members: Use simple sentences to describe family (e.g., "She is my mom.")
Asking about family: Respond to basic questions about family members (e.g., "Who is he?" "He is my brother.")
sociolinguistic: Describing Family Roles: Using appropriate language to describe family members and their roles (e.g., "This is my mother.").

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Family Portrait
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students draw a family portrait, labeling each family member with sentences like "This is my mom." and "He is my brother."

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Family Member Interview
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students ask a family member simple questions like "Who is she/he?" and record their answers to share with the class.

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Digital Family Tree
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students create a family tree using a digital family tree maker or a slide presentation app, including simple sentences like "This is my dad." and answers to questions such as "Who is she?" with "She is my grandmother."

skills: Emotional Awareness: Identifying family relationships and describing them using simple words.
Communication: Using personal pronouns and possessive adjectives to identify family members.
technology: Create a digital family tree using a simple app with photos and names.

scenarios.category: Expressing Ourselves and Connecting with Others

listening.general_standard: Can identify sounds of familiar words and basic phrases spoken slowly and clearly.
specific_standards: Receptive: Catch the main idea of a short, simple story or announcement with visual support or illustrations.
Interactive: Identify objects in the immediate environment in response to simple questions.
learning_outcomes: Can recognize colors and transportation-related words, such as "car" and "bus," in simple announcements with visual aids (e.g., circling the picture of a "red car" when it is mentioned in a short visual announcement).
Can follow simple instructions related to transportation items as heard in simple stories (e.g., "Find the green car.").
Can respond to simple questions about transportation vehicles by pointing or naming them (e.g., "What color is the car?").

reading.general_standard: Can recognize familiar words in simple texts.
specific_standards: Reading: Sequence pictures to demonstrate comprehension of texts that include a clear beginning, middle, and end.
Reading: Show comprehension of the main action or event in a very simple storybook with pictures.
Phonemic Awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.
learning_outcomes: Can sequence pictures to show a clear beginning, middle, and end of the action in a simple story (e.g., "Marta sees the red bus." "The red bus stops." "People exit the bus.").
Can show comprehension of the main action or event in a simple storybook by pointing to pictures.

Can identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound (e.g., identifying the sound /y/ in the word "yellow" when shown a picture of a yellow taxi).

speaking.general_standard: Can use basic language and formulaic expressions to describe things and answer questions.
specific_standards: Productive: Name common objects and people, including family members.
Interactive: Take part in very simple classroom games using one or two repeated lines.
learning_outcomes: Can name transportation vehicles using color words (e.g., "That's a green car.").
Can answer simple questions about transportation and colors using one or two words (e.g., "What color is the car?").
Can participate in classroom activities involving transportation vocabulary (e.g., "naming vehicles").

writing.general_standard: Can write about oneself using simple words.
specific_standards: Productive: Fill in a form with their name and age with adult help.
Interactive: Write very simple sentences with assistance, even if errors are present.
learning_outcomes: Can complete a simple form with teacher assistance, filling in details about transportation items (e.g., writing "red" for the color of a bus).
Can write simple words independently to label transportation items on drawings (e.g., "bus," "car").
Can write very simple sentences with assistance, even if errors are present (e.g., "This is a green car.").

mediation.general_standard: Can use mediation strategies such as gestures and body language to express basic ideas.
specific_standards: Text: Draw a single picture that represents a simple story or event.
Communication: Lead a simple classroom activity like a lineup or cleanup using gestures and basic words.
learning_outcomes: Can use gestures or drawings to describe transportation vehicles in the classroom (e.g., drawing a picture of a car or miming the act of driving a car to describe the word "car").
Can clarify responses by pointing to pictures of transportation vehicles when needed.
Can use basic words and body language to explain how transportation vehicles are used during classroom activities (e.g., pretending to drive by moving hands like holding a steering wheel).

grammatical_features: Present simple (e.g., "It is a red bike.")

pronunciation_and_phonemic_awareness.phonemic_awareness: Initial sound at the beginning of vehicle-related vocabulary (e.g., /k/ in "car") and identification of sounds of letters of the alphabet in familiar vocabulary (e.g., /y/ in "yellow")

pragmatic: Describing objects: When describing colors in transportation, use phrases that describe vehicles, like "It is a red bike." "That is a green car." or "It's a blue bus."
Answering questions about colors and objects: Respond with one-word answers or gestures for vehicles and colors (e.g., "Point to the blue bus.")
sociolinguistic: Norms of politeness for making requests: Using culturally appropriate norms of politeness in communication when describing transportation (e.g., "Please, point to the blue car.").

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Vehicle Color Matching
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students create a matching game by drawing and coloring different vehicles (e.g., green cars and red buses) and matching them to color labels.

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Vehicle Drawing Game
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students draw and color vehicles (cars, buses, bikes) and label them with sentences like "This is a red bus.".

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Transportation Chart
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students create a transportation chart with columns for vehicles and colors, labeling each with sentences like "That is a blue car.".

skills: Creativity: Describing transportation items using descriptive words creatively.
technology: Use a tablet to play a color and vehicle matching game or design one digitally.

scenarios.category: Expressing Ourselves and Connecting with Others

listening.general_standard: Can identify sounds of familiar words and basic phrases spoken slowly and clearly.
specific_standards: Receptive: Recognize simple classroom instructions spoken slowly and clearly.
Interactive: Respond to simple conversations on familiar topics by using gestures or by pointing.
learning_outcomes: Can follow simple classroom instructions about shapes using gestures or actions (e.g., "Point to the square." "Find the triangle.").
Can point to and name shapes during activities when prompted (e.g., "What is this?" Student points to a circle and says, "circle").
Can respond to very simple shape-related commands by pointing.

reading.general_standard: Can recognize familiar words in simple texts.
specific_standards: Reading: Recognize familiar words in simple texts.
Reading: Match simple words to corresponding pictures.
Phonemic Awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.
learning_outcomes: Can point to pictures of shapes when prompted during reading activities (e.g., pointing to a picture of a triangle in a storybook when asked, "What is this shape?").
Can match simple shape-related words to pictures (e.g., "square," "circle").
Can produce the sound of the alphabet letters when supported by a concrete item or picture representing the sound (e.g., /s/ in "square").

speaking.general_standard: Can use basic language and formulaic expressions to describe things and answer questions.
specific_standards: Productive: Name common classroom objects and familiar people.
Interactive: Engage with very simple classroom commands.
learning_outcomes: Can identify and name common shapes and colors in daily classroom routines with teacher guidance (e.g., "This is a circle." "It is blue.").
Can use simple greetings and farewells in a storytelling activity involving shapes and colors (e.g., "Hello, red circle!" "Goodbye, blue square!").
Can point to and name familiar classroom shapes and colors during activities with teacher prompts.

writing.general_standard: Can write about oneself using simple words.
specific_standards: Productive: Write numbers up to ten with guidance.
Interactive: Write one-word answers to simple questions.
learning_outcomes: Can write numbers and shape-related words with teacher guidance (e.g., counting the number of squares in a picture and writing the number and the shape's name below the picture).
Can label pictures of shapes with simple words like "square" or "circle."
Can write simple one-word answers to questions about shapes with support (e.g., writing the word "circle" in response to the question, "What shape is this?").

mediation.general_standard: Can use mediation strategies such as gestures and body language to express basic ideas.
specific_standards: Communication: Use gestures and demonstrations to help classmates perform a simple task.
Concept: Use a word and a gesture or drawing to describe a familiar object.
learning_outcomes: Can use gestures and simple words to describe shapes to classmates (e.g., using hands to represent the shape of a circle).
Can use a combination of drawings and words to clarify their responses about shapes (e.g., drawing the shape of a triangle and writing the word next to it).
Can describe shapes using body language and pointing during classroom activities (e.g., miming a square shape by using the arms to create straight lines).

pronunciation_and_phonemic_awareness.phonemic_awareness: Shape names, initial sounds in names of shapes (e.g., /s/ in "square")

pragmatic: Identifying shapes: Recognize and name shapes like squares and circles during classroom activities
Describing shapes: Use simple phrases to talk about shapes (e.g., "This is a rectangle.")
sociolinguistic: Shape and color recognition: Describing characteristics of objects around using the names of shapes and colors (e.g., "The sun is yellow.").

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Shape Collage
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Children cut out pictures of objects in different shapes (circles, squares, rectangles) and create a collage, labeling each shape with sentences like "This is a circle."

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Shape Sorting Activity
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students sort shapes (e.g., squares, circles) into groups and describe each with simple sentences like "This is a square."

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Digital Shape Game
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students play a digital shape recognition game, matching objects to their corresponding shapes and labeling them.

skills: Shape Recognition: Identifying and labeling shapes.
technology: Use a digital collage-making tool or an interactive shape recognition app.

listening.general_standard: Can identify sounds of familiar words and basic phrases spoken slowly and clearly.
specific_standards: Receptive: Recognize familiar words and basic phrases.
Interactive: Identify objects in the immediate environment in response to simple questions.
learning_outcomes: Can recognize simple sentences about food shapes (e.g., "Oranges are round.").
Can follow basic instructions involving food shapes (e.g., "Find the round apple.").
Can respond to questions about food shapes using words or gestures (e.g., "What shape is the cracker?" "A square.").

reading.general_standard: Can recognize familiar words in simple texts.
specific_standards: Reading: Recognize familiar words in simple texts.
Reading: Identify simple words for very common symbols.
Phonemic Awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.
learning_outcomes: Can point to and name familiar food and shape-related words in simple texts (e.g., recognizing the word "apple" in the sentence, "The apple is round.").
Can identify simple words related to shapes (e.g., pointing to a cracker and saying "square").

Can recognize sounds of letters related to the names of food items when supported with visuals (e.g., identifying the /ɔ/ sound in "orange" along with the letter O, or the /b/ sound in "banana" when shown its crescent shape).

speaking.general_standard: Can use basic language and formulaic expressions to describe things and answer questions.
specific_standards: Productive: Name primary and secondary colors.
Interactive: Answer questions using simple words.
learning_outcomes: Can identify and name primary and secondary colors when describing the color and shape of food items (e.g., "This is a red apple." "The brown cracker is square.").
Can answer simple questions about both colors and shapes using one or two words (e.g., "What shape is the orange?" "It's round.").
Can use gestures to support responses about colors and shapes of food items (e.g., pointing to a round object when asked to find a shape).

writing.general_standard: Can write about oneself using simple words.
specific_standards: Productive: Label common items using a model to copy from.
Interactive: Write simple phrases with errors on a drawing or postcard.
learning_outcomes: Can label food items in drawings with simple words (e.g., writing "round" under a picture of an orange).
Can write simple phrases with guidance, describing food shapes (e.g., "This is a square cracker.").
Can copy simple sentences about food shapes with teacher support (e.g., copying the word "juice box" under a picture of a juice box).

mediation.general_standard: Can use mediation strategies such as gestures and body language to express basic ideas.
specific_standards: Text: Use pictures or objects to express ideas.
Concept: Use facial expressions and simple gestures to help explain feelings or needs.
learning_outcomes: Can use gestures or body language to describe food items (e.g., pretending to hold a round apple).
Can clarify responses using pictures or pointing to food-related visuals (e.g., showing the picture of a cracker to clarify its meaning).
Can use facial expressions and gestures to help describe food preferences and shapes (e.g., pretending to take a bite of an apple and using the hands to show that it is round).

pronunciation_and_phonemic_awareness.phonemic_awareness: Recognizing sounds of letters of the alphabet in names of shapes and foods (e.g., /æ/ in "apple")

pragmatic: Identifying food shapes: Identifying and describing foods by shape (e.g., "The cracker is square.").
Asking about shapes in food: Engage in simple questions about food items (e.g., "What shape is the apple?")
sociolinguistic: Describing food: Using simple words to describe the colors and shapes of fruits and other foods, such as "orange" and "cracker".

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Food Shape Match
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students collect pictures of different foods and match them to their shapes (e.g., round oranges, square crackers), creating a poster or chart.

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Food Shape Sorting
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students sort different foods into shape categories (e.g., round, square) and label them with simple sentences like "This is a round orange."

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Digital Food Shape Chart
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students create a digital food shape chart, labeling each food with sentences like "The cracker is square."

skills: Problem-Solving: Identifying shapes in everyday foods.
technology: Use an app to take pictures of foods and match them to shapes digitally.

listening.general_standard: Can identify sounds of familiar words and basic phrases spoken slowly and clearly.
specific_standards: Receptive: Catch the main idea of a short, simple story or announcement with visual support or illustrations.
Interactive: Respond to simple conversations on familiar topics by using gestures or by pointing.
learning_outcomes: Can recognize nature-related words like "tree," "flowers," and "bushes" in simple conversations or stories (e.g., "The tree is tall." "The flowers are pink.").
Can follow basic instructions related to outdoor environments in conversations (e.g., "Look at the tall tree.").
Can respond to questions about nature by pointing or using gestures (e.g., pointing to a tree in response to the question "Do you see a tree?").

reading.general_standard: Can recognize familiar words in simple texts.
specific_standards: Reading: Match simple words to corresponding pictures.
Reading: Identify simple words for very common symbols.
Phonemic Awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.
learning_outcomes: Can match nature-related words with primary or secondary colors to corresponding pictures (e.g., "pink flowers," "green tree," "yellow leaves").
Can recognize written words for nature symbols when supported by pictures (e.g., leaves, seeds).

Can recognize sounds of letters related to nature-related vocabulary when supported with visuals (e.g., identifying the /s/ sound in "sun" when shown a picture of the sun or the /t/ sound in "tree" when shown an image of a tree).

speaking.general_standard: Can use basic language and formulaic expressions to describe things and answer questions.
specific_standards: Productive: Name primary and secondary colors.
Interactive: Express likes or dislikes using simple words and pointing.
learning_outcomes: Can identify and name colors of familiar nature objects (e.g., "green trees," "pink flowers," or "yellow leaves").
Can respond to simple questions about nature with words or gestures (e.g., "What is this?" Student points and says, "tree.").
Can express likes or dislikes about nature-related objects using simple words and pointing (e.g., "I like trees" while pointing to a tree).

writing.general_standard: Can write about oneself using simple words.
specific_standards: Productive: Write simple greetings on cards.
Interactive: Write very simple sentences with assistance, even if errors are present.
learning_outcomes: Can write simple greetings or nature-related phrases on cards (e.g., "Happy garden day!").
Can write simple sentences about the garden or nature with teacher support (e.g., "This is a tall tree").
Can label pictures of plants or trees using simple words like "flowers," "trees," and "leaves".

mediation.general_standard: Can use mediation strategies such as gestures and body language to express basic ideas.
specific_standards: Communication: Use gestures and demonstrations to help classmates perform a simple task.
Concept: Use a word and a gesture or drawing to describe a familiar object.
learning_outcomes: Can use gestures or body language to describe plants and trees to classmates (e.g., miming being a tree by stretching the arms upward or pretending to hold a bouquet of flowers).
Can clarify responses about nature by pointing to garden objects or drawing pictures (e.g., drawing a simple flower or a tree on the board).
Can use simple gestures or drawings to explain feelings about the garden (e.g., pointing to a tall tree in response to the question, "What do you like most in the garden?").

grammatical_features: Noun + verb + adjective (e.g., "The flowers are pink.")
Present simple (e.g., "This is a tall tree.")

pronunciation_and_phonemic_awareness.phonemic_awareness: Identification of sounds of letters of the alphabet in nature-related vocabulary (e.g., /s/ in "sun")

pragmatic: Talking about nature: Describe plants and trees with singular and plural phrases (e.g., "The tree is tall." "The flowers are pink.")
Asking questions in nature: Use basic questions like "What color is the flower?" to learn about plants
sociolinguistic: Respect for nature: Talking about plants and trees using respectful language (e.g., "I like plants.").

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Garden Nature Journal
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Children explore the school garden, observe plants and trees, and record their findings in a nature journal, labeling each plant with simple sentences like "This is a tall tree."

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Garden Scavenger Hunt
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students participate in a scavenger hunt in the school garden, finding objects like pink flowers and tall trees and labeling them with sentences.

twenty_first_century_projects.title: Digital Nature Photo Book
twenty_first_century_projects.overview: Students take photos of plants and trees in the school garden and create a digital photo book, labeling each plant with simple sentences like "This is a pink flower."

skills: Observation: Learning about plants and nature.
technology: Use a nature journaling app to record findings and add digital photos.
